new_classes.definition_en: Water in its gaseous state (steam), classified under CN subheading 2201 90 00 as an 'other' product of heading 2201.

new_classes.label_en: treated natural water (filtered, sterilised, purified or softened)
new_classes.label_de: behandeltes natürliches Wasser (filtriert, entkeimtes, geklärtes oder entkalktes)
new_classes.definition_en: Natural water that has been subjected to one or more treatment processes — filtering, sterilisation, purification, or softening — rendering it distinct from natural mineral water and plain potable water; classified under CN subheading 2201 90 00.

new_classes.label_en: water treatment process (filtering, sterilisation, purification or softening)
new_classes.definition_en: A process applied to natural water comprising one or more of the following steps: filtering, sterilising, purifying, or softening, used to distinguish treated natural water (CN 2201 90 00) from untreated or mineral water.
Water in solid form (ice or snow), referenced in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201 paragraph (A), classified under CN subheading 2201 90 00.

new_classes.label_en: distilled or conductivity water and water of similar purity
new_classes.label_de: destilliertes Wasser, Leitwasser oder Wasser ähnlicher Reinheit
new_classes.definition_en: Highly purified water including distilled water, deionised water, and water of similar purity, referenced in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201 paragraph (D), classified under CN subheading 2201 90 00.

coverage_explanation: The axioms cover the four main product categories referenced in CN 2201 90 00: (1) water vapour / steam, (2) treated natural water (filtered, sterilised, purified, softened), (3) ice and snow (HS EN paragraph A), and (4) distilled / conductivity water (HS EN paragraph D). The treatment process class captures the filtering/sterilising/purifying/softening distinction that separates treated water from mineral water (2201 10) and plain water. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the HS Explanatory Note paragraphs A and D are referenced by the legal text but their full content is not provided here — additional products from those paragraphs may exist that are not yet modelled; (b) the disjointness between WaterVapour, IceAndSnow, TreatedNaturalWater, and DistilledOrConductivityWater is only partially expressed via complement restrictions rather than full mutual disjointness; (c) DistilledOrConductivityWater does not yet have a restriction linking it to a production/purification process class.
